title: Train, validation, test and leakage

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Train, validation, test and leakage; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: scikit-learn User Guide; 1.9.x target, accessed 2026-07; User Guide sections 3 and 7: model selection, pipelines, preprocessing, imputation, and leakage-safe evaluation; atomic topic: Train, validation, test and leakage
sources.claim: Train data fits parameters, validation or cross-validation guides choices, and a held-out test estimates the frozen procedure; splits must reflect future deployment units, groups, time, and duplication structure.

sources.claim: Train data fits parameters, validation or cross-validation guides choices, and a held-out test estimates the frozen procedure; splits must reflect future deployment units, groups, time, and duplication structure.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Train data fits parameters, validation or cross-validation guides choices, and a held-out test estimates the frozen procedure; splits must reflect future deployment units, groups, time, and duplication structure.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: All records for one patient stay in one split and the newest time period is held out when deployment predicts future visits. Your goal is to choose a split strategy and detect target, temporal, group or preprocessing leakage. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: python quality project; Problem and environment characteristics; Features and preprocessing; Problem, target and success measure. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: Train data fits parameters, validation or cross-validation guides choices, and a held-out test estimates the frozen procedure; splits must reflect future deployment units, groups, time, and duplication structure. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: Random rows can leak people or future time, repeated test inspection turns test into validation, preprocessing can leak, a small test has uncertainty, and distribution shift can make any historical estimate optimistic. The learner must reject this overclaim: Any random 80/20 split prevents leakage as long as the target column is removed from model inputs.

includes: Split according to deployment structure and keep test information outside training and tuning.
Choose a split strategy and detect target, temporal, group or preprocessing leakage.
excludes: Random rows can leak people or future time, repeated test inspection turns test into validation, preprocessing can leak, a small test has uncertainty, and distribution shift can make any historical estimate optimistic.

outcomes: Choose a split strategy and detect target, temporal, group or preprocessing leakage.

examples.title: Train, validation, test and leakage: bounded direct case
examples.situation: All records for one patient stay in one split and the newest time period is held out when deployment predicts future visits.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: All records for one patient stay in one split and the newest time period is held out when deployment predicts future visits.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Split by the deployment unit and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.
examples.result: This example supports only the atomic outcome because Split by the deployment unit and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.

examples.title: Train, validation, test and leakage: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: Near-duplicate documents are grouped before splitting so retrieval or classification cannot score by memorizing another copy.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: Near-duplicate documents are grouped before splitting so retrieval or classification cannot score by memorizing another copy.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: Random rows can leak people or future time, repeated test inspection turns test into validation, preprocessing can leak, a small test has uncertainty, and distribution shift can make any historical estimate optimistic.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: Any random 80/20 split prevents leakage as long as the target column is removed from model inputs.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Choose a split strategy and detect target, temporal, group or preprocessing leakage.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Identify the first unsupported inference in this belief and the evidence required to narrow it: Any random 80/20 split prevents leakage as long as the target column is removed from model inputs.

limitations: clean procedures cannot recover absent populations, authorize data, or make a harmful target appropriate

recap.summary: You should now be able to choose a split strategy and detect target, temporal, group or preprocessing leakage. Keep this boundary in view: Random rows can leak people or future time, repeated test inspection turns test into validation, preprocessing can leak, a small test has uncertainty, and distribution shift can make any historical estimate optimistic.
